Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, gTMT family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. gTMT family. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU00914}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=ajmaline + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 4-methylajmaline + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:79631, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:58567, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:230509; EC=2.1.1.393; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:27122470}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:79632; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:27122470}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=norajmaline + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 4-methylnorajmaline + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:79627, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:77618, ChEBI:CHEBI:230510; EC=2.1.1.393; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:27122470}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:79628; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:27122470};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: N-methyltransferase involved in the biosynthesis of ajmaline-type monoterpenoid indole alkaloids (MIAs) natural products, important plant-derived pharmaceuticals used in the therapy of heart disorders (PubMed:27122470). Catalyzes the indole N-methylation of ajmaline to produce 4-methylajmaline (PubMed:27122470). Also able, with a lower efficiency, to mediate the conversion of norajmaline to 4-methylnorajmaline (PubMed:27122470). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:27122470}.
